creator: aryeh.friedman@gmail.com
text:
If you go back in the aegis-devel archives you will see I had the 
identical issue in my case it turned out to something weird with how 
some dependant port was configured I never did track it down I just 
rm'ed /var/db/ports and did a portupgrade -af and used all default 
options.... see if that works but only after you try the below

Try to see if you can replicate this as much as possible:

flosoft# cd /usr/ports/devel/aegis
flosoft# make deinstall
===>  Deinstalling for devel/aegis
===>   Deinstalling aegis-4.24_1
flosoft# cd ..
flosoft# rm -rf aegis/
flosoft# !cvsu
cvsu: Event not found.
flosoft# rm -rf /usr/ports/distfiles/aegis-4.24.tar.gz
flosoft# cvsup -h cvsup.us.freebsd.org 
/usr/share/examples/cvsup/cvs-supfile
 
[lots of output]

flosoft#  /usr/ports
flosoft# cvs -q update -dP

[lots of output]

flosoft# cd devel/aegis
flosoft# uname -a

12:36:53 EDT 2008
